content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `origin/master` and read the changed `daemon_loop.rs` and `engine.rs` sections end-to-end. I found one fix-now correctness regression in the new fail-closed reconciliation flow.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `persist_workflow_run_id` only updates the task record on disk, but both dispatch paths still pass the old `active_task` into `try_reconcile_success`, so newly completed tasks are guaranteed to look unbound on their first success path.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs
remediation_hint: After `persist_workflow_run_id(...)` at `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:992-993` / `1682-1683`, reload the task from the store or thread the captured `run_id` directly into reconciliation before calling `try_reconcile_success`. Add an end-to-end regression test for a first-time successful run.
evidence:
- `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1092-1093` and `1719-1720` call `try_reconcile_success(..., &active_task)` immediately after persistence.
- `src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:634-643` shows `set_workflow_run_id` mutates only the stored copy.
- `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1917-1920` now fail-closes whenever the in-memory task still has `workflow_run_id == None`.
impact: any task whose `workflow_run_id` was absent before dispatch will now be marked `reconciliation_no_run_binding` and push the milestone controller to `NeedsOperator` even when the authoritative run id was just persisted successfully.
   Rationale: This turns the normal happy path into a false failure for first-time completions and retryable resumes that started without a stored binding, so the daemon will incorrectly require operator intervention on otherwise successful runs.
